title: Englerophoenix regia
intro: Englerophoenix regia, commonly known as the Indian crownshaft palm, is a species of palm tree endemic to the Western Ghats in India. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Englerophoenix regia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Indian medicine, Englerophoenix regia is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and sore throats.
taxonomy: Englerophoenix regia is classified as a species of palm tree, specifically within the family Arecaceae. It belongs to the genus Englerophoenix, which consists of a single species. The genus Englerophoenix is further classified within the subfamily Arecoideae. As a member of the Arecaceae family, Englerophoenix regia shares characteristics with other palm trees. Its classification as a species within the Englerophoenix genus helps to identify and distinguish it from other palm species.
